ExecutiveSummary: Following a recent deployment of the OrderService, a significant increase in order placement latency and error rates was observed, indicating degraded service health. Investigations reveal that the deployment included an invalid container image reference, leading to operational failures.
LikelyCause: The deployment of an invalid or non-existent container image caused the service to experience elevated error rates and latency issues.
ImmediateAction: Confirm whether the latest deployment changed the container image or startup configuration, and validate application health and endpoint error rate.
RollbackRecommendation: Revert traffic to the previous healthy revision and cease further rollout until the error rate has normalized.
ValidationPlan: Verify that the error rate is below 1%, check that p95 latency is below 500 ms, and ensure there are no new deployment failures logged in CI/CD.
StakeholderMessage: Attention: The deployment of the OrderService has caused increased latency and elevated error rates. Immediate actions are being taken to roll back to a previous stable version while an investigation into the deployment issues is conducted.
